stories.title: A Friendly Competition
sentences: Zhang Peng has a new PE teacher, Mr. Jones.
Mr. Jones is very young and tall.
What is he like?
He is funny and very kind to the students.
Today, they have a basketball game.
Zhang Peng is hard-working on the sports field.
Is he clever?
Yes, he is a smart player too.
But Mr. Jones is very strict in the game.
Everyone must follow the rules.
They are very happy after class.

questions.question: Mr. Jones is an old teacher.
questions.explanation: The story says Mr. Jones is very young.

questions.question: What is Mr. Jones like in the basketball game?
questions.explanation: The text states 'Mr. Jones is very strict in the game' because everyone must follow the rules.

questions.question: Zhang Peng is _______ on the sports field.
questions.explanation: The text explicitly describes Zhang Peng as hard-working.

stories.title: The Quiet Girl and the Sunny Day
sentences: Lily is a new student in our class.
She is very quiet and shy.
Our Chinese teacher is Ms. Wang.
Is she strict?
No, she is very polite and helpful.
Ms. Wang asks Lily to help the baby at the school gate.
Lily carries the heavy bag for the baby's mother.
It is a sunny day.
Lily is very helpful and kind.
We all like our new friend Lily.
She is a clever girl.

questions.question: Lily is a quiet and shy girl.
questions.explanation: The story says Lily is very quiet and shy.

questions.question: What is the Chinese teacher like?
questions.explanation: The text says Ms. Wang is not strict, but is polite and helpful.

questions.question: The weather is _______ in the story.
questions.explanation: The text mentions 'It is a sunny day'.

stories.title: Oliver's Busy Monday
sentences: Today is Monday and Oliver is very happy.
He has a busy schedule at school.
In the morning, he has English and Chinese classes.
Oliver likes English because he can read many stories.
After lunch, he has a PE class on the playground.
On Mondays, he often plays football with his friends.
He sees a green tree and some beef in the canteen.
After school, he drinks some tea and feels relaxed.
The tea is hot but it tastes very good.
Oliver loves his school life on Mondays.

questions.question: Oliver has a PE class in the morning.
questions.explanation: The story says he has PE after lunch, not in the morning.

questions.question: What does Oliver often do on Mondays after school?
questions.explanation: The story mentions he drinks some tea after school to feel relaxed.

questions.question: Oliver likes English because he can ______ many stories.
questions.explanation: The text states: 'Oliver likes English because he can read many stories.'

stories.title: Sarah's Fun Weekend
sentences: It is Saturday today and the weather is sunny.
Sarah does not go to school on the weekend.
What does she do on Saturdays?
She often cleans her room in the morning.
Then she helps her mother wash the dirty feet.
In the afternoon, Sarah and her brother go to the park.
They like to sit under the tree and eat bread.
Sometimes they read books together on the grass.
Sarah is very helpful and hardworking at home.
She is tired but she has a great time.

questions.question: Sarah goes to school on Saturdays.
questions.explanation: The story says she does not go to school on the weekend.

questions.question: What does Sarah often do in the morning?
options: She eats bread in the park.
She reads books with her brother.
questions.explanation: The story states: 'She often cleans her room in the morning.'

questions.question: Where does Sarah eat bread in the afternoon?
questions.explanation: The text says 'They like to sit under the tree and eat bread' in the afternoon.

stories.title: A Picnic with Little Cow
sentences: Little Cow lives in a small white house in the forest.
Today is sunny and the snow is melting on the hill.
He wants to have a picnic with his friends.
They sit on the green grass under a big tree.
His friend Rabbit asks, 'What would you like to eat?'
Little Cow says, 'I would like some sandwiches and salad.'
The salad is very fresh and healthy.
Rabbit likes sweet food, so she brings some red apples.
They eat and drink under the warm sun.
Everything is delicious and they are very happy today.
vocabulary_used: What would you like to eat

questions.question: Little Cow lives in a small white house.
questions.explanation: The first sentence mentions Little Cow lives in a small white house.

questions.question: What would Little Cow like for the picnic?
questions.explanation: The story states Little Cow says, 'I would like some sandwiches and salad.'

questions.question: Rabbit brings some red apples because she likes ________ food.
questions.explanation: The story says 'Rabbit likes sweet food, so she brings some red apples.'

stories.title: Dinner at Grandfather's House
sentences: Sarah goes to her grandfather's house for dinner on Friday.
Grandfather is a great cook and makes many dishes.
He looks at Sarah and asks, 'What's your favourite food?'
Sarah says, 'I love beef and potatoes best.'
Grandfather cooks some beef and many potatoes in a big pot.
The beef is a little hot but very delicious.
Grandfather also makes some vegetable soup for Sarah.
The soup is healthy because it is not too salty.
Sarah eats a lot and feels very full.
She thanks her grandfather for the wonderful dinner.

questions.question: When does Sarah go to her grandfather's house?
questions.explanation: The first sentence says Sarah goes there on Friday.

questions.question: The beef is cold and not tasty.
questions.explanation: The story says the beef is a little hot but very delicious.

questions.question: The soup is healthy because it is not too ________.
questions.explanation: The story mentions the soup is healthy because it is not too salty.

stories.title: The Talent Show Plan
sentences: Today is Monday and the school has a talent show.
Zhang Peng can do kung fu very well.
Sarah can sing English songs and she likes music.
Robin wants to help them for the show.
Robin can speak English, but he can't play the pipa.
Chen Jie says she can play the pipa.
They want to practice in the music room together.
They are very busy but they are happy.
Zhang Peng can also play basketball after school.
He does not play the basketball, he plays basketball.

questions.question: Zhang Peng is good at kung fu.
questions.explanation: The story says 'Zhang Peng can do kung fu very well'.

questions.question: What can Robin do?
questions.explanation: The story mentions 'Robin can speak English, but he can't play the pipa'.

questions.question: Chen Jie can ______ ______ ______.
questions.explanation: The story states: 'Chen Jie says she can play the pipa'.

stories.title: A Cool Day in the Library
sentences: It is a cool afternoon in the park.
Wu Binbin looks at a book under a big tree.
He can draw pictures, so he draws the lake.
His friend Oliver comes and plays football near him.
Oliver can play football, but he can't dance.
Wu Binbin says, 'I can't play football, but I can cook.'
He can make delicious food for his friends.
They see a sign on the grass.
The sign says, 'Please keep the park clean.'
They are good students and they go home together.

questions.question: Oliver can dance very well.
questions.explanation: The text says 'Oliver can play football, but he can't dance'.

questions.question: What does Wu Binbin do under the tree?
questions.explanation: The story says 'He can draw pictures, so he draws the lake'.

questions.question: Wu Binbin can ________ delicious food.
questions.explanation: The story states 'He can make delicious food for his friends'.

stories.title: A New Room for Tim
sentences: Tim has a new bedroom and it is very big.
There is a big bed near the window.
There is a blue clock on the wall.
Beside the clock, there is a beautiful photo of his family.
There is a wooden desk in front of the bed.
A water bottle is on the desk.
Tim likes to paint on his desk every Monday.
Today it is rainy, but Tim is happy in his room.
He can wait for his friends to play games here.
There are many plants near the door.

questions.question: The clock is on the wall.
questions.explanation: The story says 'There is a blue clock on the wall.'

questions.question: Where is the family photo?
questions.explanation: The story mentions 'Beside the clock, there is a beautiful photo of his family.'

questions.question: What is in front of the bed?
questions.explanation: The text states 'There is a wooden desk in front of the bed.'

stories.title: Monkey's Forest House
sentences: Little Monkey lives in a small house in the forest.
There is a photo of a banana tree on the wall.
There are many big trees behind his house.
There is a green water bottle on the floor.
A small clock is beside the water bottle.
Monkey stays at home because it is a rainy day.
He wants to eat, but he must wait for his mother.
There is a big bed with a soft pillow.
Monkey likes his house because it is very clean.
It is Monday and he is very happy today.

questions.question: Monkey stays at home because it is sunny.
questions.explanation: The story says he stays at home because it is a rainy day.

questions.question: What is behind the house?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states 'There are many big trees behind his house.'

questions.question: What is beside the water bottle?
questions.explanation: The story mentions 'A small clock is beside the water bottle.'

stories.title: A Trip to the Forest
sentences: Today is a sunny day.
Zhang Peng and his friends go to a nature park.
There is a big forest in the park.
Is there a river in the forest?
Yes, there is a long river with many fish.
Is there a lake near the forest?
No, there isn't, but there is a small hill.
There is a small house on the hill.
They hear a loud sound from the house.
It is a little mouse looking for food.
They are very happy in the nature park.

questions.question: There is a big lake in the forest.
questions.explanation: The story says 'No, there isn't' when asking about a lake near the forest.

questions.question: What is in the river?
questions.explanation: The text states: 'Yes, there is a long river with many fish.'

questions.question: Where is the small house?
questions.explanation: The story mentions: 'There is a small house on the hill.'

stories.title: The Beautiful Village
sentences: Sarah lives in a small village.
The village is in a beautiful nature park.
Are there any tall buildings in the village?
No, there aren't.
But there are many trees and flowers.
Are there any mountains near the village?
Yes, there are some high mountains.
The clouds over the mountains are very white.
Sarah likes to walk in the mountains.
The air is fresh and the birds sing.
She loves her quiet village life.

questions.question: There are many tall buildings in Sarah's village.
questions.explanation: The story says 'No, there aren't' when asking about tall buildings.

questions.question: What can you see over the mountains?
questions.explanation: The text says: 'The clouds over the mountains are very white.'

questions.question: What are there near the village?
questions.explanation: The story states: 'Yes, there are some high mountains' near the village.
